The number of pre-deployed DNN models at the RSUs

Fig. 5. Performance comparisons among algorithms for different number of pre-deployed DNN models at the RSUs

ular trend as the number of RSUs equipped with GPUs varies, because neither algorithm takes into account the processing of tasks with GPUs. However, the overall trend of the proposed LAMA-GAP gradually decreases, except for the last data point, where no decline is observed. This may be because, when all RSUs are equipped with GPUs, frequent task migration between RSUs leads to performance degradation. Compared to the baseline algorithms, our proposed approach demonstrates a superior ability to efficiently utilize GPU resources.

Fig. 5 shows the performance comparisons between the proposed algorithm LAMA-GAP and baseline algorithms BA-MA and BA-GR, in terms of the maximum load of RSUs, for different numbers of pre-deployed DNN models at the RSUs. From Fig. 5, our proposed LAMA-GAP always performs better than algorithms BA-MA and BA-GR. For example, for the case where the number of pre-deployed DNN models is 3, the proposed LAMA-GAP can achieve an improvement of 39.4% and 33.7% compared with baseline BA-MA and BA-GR, respectively. Moreover, as the number of pre-deployed DNN models on the RSUs
